title: Cayratia wrayi
intro: Cayratia wrayi, also known as the Chinese grape or snake fruit vine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Vitaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant to protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Cayratia wrayi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Cayratia wrayi is often prepared as a decoction, wine, or dried fruit, and is used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Cayratia wrayi is classified in the family Vitaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the grape or wine family. This family includes around 14 genera and over 900 species. Cayratia wrayi is specifically a species within the genus Cayratia, which is native to Southeast Asia. The genus Cayratia is part of the larger family Vitaceae, which is also home to other well-known species such as Vitis and Cissus. Cayratia wrayi is a climber or a vine, and its classification as a member of the Vitaceae family reflects its characteristics and growth habits.
